title: Scientists Found A Better Language For AI Agents
keyIdeas:
**2-sentence summary:**  
A new research paper proposes that AI agents should communicate directly via raw latent state vectors (cross-agent latent state transfer) instead of natural language, which drastically reduces token usage and improves performance. On competition-level math problems, this method boosted accuracy from 73% to 86% while cutting token consumption by 75% and costing only $4 to train—though it has so far only been tested on smaller models.

**Key takeaways:**  
- Traditional multi-agent systems waste computation because each agent must decode and re-encode full sentences; the new method passes undecoded latent states between agents.  
- On difficult math benchmarks, the approach improved accuracy from 73% to 86% using sub-10 billion parameter models.  
- Token usage dropped by 75%, making the system far more efficient.  
- Training cost was just $4 (coffee money), yet the results rival much larger, expensive models.  
- Controlled experiments confirmed the gain comes from the architecture, not just from a better teacher model.  
- The method introduces a potential new scaling law: more rounds of latent communication yield better results.  
- Limitations: tested only on small models, and there is an optimal latent thought length of around 80 steps per round.  

**Notable quotes, numbers, and claims:**  
- *“They all evaporated into the latent space. Loving it.”*  
- *“Four bucks. Basically, you spend your coffee money on these agents and in return they punch a hole in space-time.”*  
- Accuracy jump: 73% → 86% on competition-level math questions.  
- Token usage reduction: 75%.  
- Training cost: $4.  
- Optimal latent thought length: ~80 steps.
